title: Nordson Corporation (NDSN) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Nordson Corporation (NDSN) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: An 8.0% free cash flow growth rate reflects Nordson's historical ability to compound earnings through a mix of organic growth (pricing power and volume) and strategic acquisitions, particularly as it expands its higher-growth medical device segment.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate is appropriate for an industrial equipment manufacturer. While Nordson has a strong recurring revenue base (parts and service), it still carries cyclical exposure to end markets like consumer electronics, warranting a moderate risk premium.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal growth rate assumes the company will grow slightly faster than long-term inflation, driven by global megatrends in automation, nearshoring, and rising living standards requiring more packaged goods and medical devices.

thesisTitle: Premium Price for a Premium Compounder
thesisParagraphs: Nordson is a textbook example of a high-quality industrial compounder. Its core business model—selling highly engineered dispensing equipment that becomes deeply embedded in a customer's manufacturing process—creates immense switching costs. This installed base then generates a highly profitable, recurring stream of revenue through parts, consumables, and service. This 'razor-and-blade' model allows Nordson to generate excellent returns on invested capital and robust free cash flow, currently running above $514 million.

However, quality rarely comes cheap. While the underlying business fundamentals are outstanding, the current market valuation appears slightly stretched. The market is pricing in near-perfection regarding execution and successful integration of future acquisitions. Our DCF model suggests that while Nordson is a fantastic business, the stock is currently trading at a premium to its intrinsic value, offering no margin of safety for new capital at these levels.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via Yahoo Finance (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is Nordson considered a 'razor-and-blade' business model?
faq.answer: Nordson sells complex dispensing equipment (the 'razor') which is often a one-time capital expense for a manufacturer. However, that equipment requires ongoing proprietary replacement parts, specialized nozzles, and service (the 'blades') to function properly, generating a recurring, high-margin revenue stream.

faq.question: What is the significance of the Medical and Fluid Solutions segment?
faq.answer: Historically tied to more cyclical industrial and electronics markets, Nordson has strategically expanded into medical devices. This segment generally offers higher margins, faster growth profiles, and less cyclicality, improving the overall quality of the company's earnings.

faq.question: If the company is so high quality, why is the verdict 'Overvalued'?
faq.answer: Valuation is a function of price relative to cash flows. While Nordson has excellent fundamentals and a wide moat, the current share price requires very aggressive growth assumptions to justify. At $267, the market is fully recognizing the quality, leaving no margin of safety if macroeconomic conditions temporarily slow industrial spending.
